messages.text:
Where there are fewer applicants, you've got better odds.
*5 regions sitters rarely visit*
On Nomador, some regions in France are less popular than Brittany or Provence. Not because they're less beautiful. Just because fewer people talk about them.
Five current examples:
ЁЯПЮя╕П *Aveyron* : stone houses, shepherd dogs, lavender in summer
ЁЯМД *Cantal* : cottages with cats, hikes right outside the door
ЁЯПб *Tarn-et-Garonne* : large family homes, several pets
ЁЯММ *Loz├иre* : silence, stars, animals roaming free
ЁЯП░ *Berry* : castles, novels to finish, cats on the windowsills
For applications, these regions have less competition. For your first sit, that's where to look.

Dear Gardyner,
You've probably seen the headlines by now: a Cyclospora outbreak
<https://mygardyn.com/blog/2026-cyclospora-outbreak/> likely tied to lettuce
has sickened close to 7,000 people across 34 states.
It's not the first time, either. Spinach one year, romaine the next, basil
after that. The names change, but the pattern doesn't: food changes hands
dozens of times and travels thousands of miles before it lands on your plate.
Somewhere in that chain, things can go wrong.
We built Gardyn to get around that chain entirely.
Your Gardyn produce is harvested minutes before you eat it, in your own
kitchen. No truck, no warehouse, no unknown hands. Just you, picking what
you're about to serve your family. ThereтАЩs relief in knowing you can stop
scanning recall lists тАУ you can trust what you grow.
That's peace of mind worth passing along. If the people you care about are
starting to think harder about where their food comes from, itтАЩs the perfect
time to share your referral code. Give $100, get $100 тАФ theyтАЩll be one step
closer to cleaner home-grown food, and youтАЩll get $100 to use on anything at
Gardyn.
Romaine, cilantro, basil, iceberg тАФ the exact greens making headlines this
week тАФ are ready to grow in your Gardyn, so you always know exactly where your
food came from.
With care,
The Gardyn Team ЁЯМ▒

** Reality Games
------------------------------------------------------------
** This is Definitely Real
------------------------------------------------------------
https://x.com/mikemorgenstern/status/2078196653954421118?utm_source=Definitely+Real+Master+List&utm_campaign=8370945735-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2026_04_28_08_12_COPY_03&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_4aeb0fd447-4a148a0cf8-599498655
Check it out: Claude made a video about how we made the deepfakes level (https://x.com/mikemorgenstern/status/2078196653954421118?utm_source=Definitely+Real+Master+List&utm_campaign=8370945735-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2026_04_28_08_12_COPY_03&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_4aeb0fd447-4a148a0cf8-599498655)
Hey all,
One of the trickiest VFX shots in our movie is the deepfakes scene near the climax. After flying into the internet as a virtual 3D environment of Cirque de Soleil style TikTok stages and nightclub influencer headquarters, our lead Zander plays the Doxbox game, collecting evidence about his best friend. And thenтАжDEEPFAKES! A beat-saber-becomes-Starfox hell realm where the evil David King overwhelms our hero and dominates the narrative.
Unlike the rest of our 3D motion, this one really needed to feel like an actual video game. So we brought it over into Claude. I got it to learn Cinema 4D and migrate our 300 object scene into 3js, running rounds of iteration. WeтАЩd already locked where we wanted the videos to land onscreen, so I had Claude create a framework that tracked locked decisions and computed them. What was wide open: the speed of the carriers, the ship movement, dodges, motion style, graphics, and all the little indicators.
After many rounds, Claude was iterating on new constraints (тАЬletтАЩs try having the player dodge twice as far, be twice as franticтАЭ) and doing a solve for them. It was a lot of kinks to iron out, but now we can adjust any one moment as well as the overall dynamics. It recomputes, ideates, researches, gives itself feedback, and shows me the multiple different ways it can fit this multidimensional curve given all our constraints.
Take a look at the video Claude made (https://x.com/mikemorgenstern/status/2078196653954421118?utm_source=Definitely+Real+Mas

Webinar Details:
ЁЯУЕ  July 29, 2026      ЁЯХУ  1:00 PM ET
Hi Julia,
Crypto mining is one of the most price-responsive load resources on the grid, fast to ramp, fast to curtail, and increasingly large enough to move market fundamentals when it does either. As crypto capacity grows, the market dynamics that follow are anything but predictable.
In the fourth and final installment (https://email.yesenergy.com/e3t/Ctc/ZV+113/d2svJC04/VX353b3JMhWBW6dsVRs1sML-3W2x7JLQ5RC1g7N5d8yQ83l5QzW7lCdLW6lZ3n7W716J2K1r-k9rVfwP5H1mvcD8W1-wPyS2HzkYKW5c6Ll56W7pfQW86-t-n4kNTq6V2ntP04X1s1fW3W0Wb23TwWqdVmXJjw5SXN_lW2LHzYl65Jtm0W31L4-W8dhR5gW39_Csm8BXp-YF1bFYk_WBZNW28gNgw3VlnkvW1fPGFY45JT7_W8VqB9d6yR4XPW2lK5NF3CM9dFW22xPCh994SqtVhK7D14rNywVN8H0tXDP77b-VRMybH1csshlW97c53t2cZ_DWN7lrKBylzswfW9gHq6X2vfTxGW103C7m61B94zf2TmTvH04 ) of the Yes Energy Summer Demand Series, Alex Bennitt, Product Manager, closes out the series by digging into what crypto mining actually looks like as a grid resource, and what happens to market conditions when gigawatts of crypto mining demand are curtailed in a matter of minutes.
You'll walk away understanding:
- Current crypto capacity levels
- What real operational data from crypto mining facilities reveals about load behavior
- How approximately 3 GW of near-simultaneous load curtailment affects market fundamentals
- What the future trajectory of this resou

https://betakit.com/general-fusion-begins-trading-on-nasdaq-becomes-first-nuclear-fusion-company-to-go-public/General?mc_cid=d4e3182122&mc_eid=UNIQID Fusion begins trading on Nasdaq, becomes first fusion company to go public
General Fusion has become the first publicly listed fusion company after completing a merger with a special purpose acquisition company (SPAC). The listing provides the company with broader access to capital as it advances its technology toward deployment.
https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-07-13/global-investment-in-fusion-companies-surges-69-to-4-5-billion?mc_cid=111d3f3090&mc_eid=UNIQIDGlobal&mc_cid=d4e3182122&mc_eid=UNIQID Investment in Fusion Companies Surges 69% to $4.5 Billion
The Fusion Industry Association released its 2026 Global Fusion Industry Report, showing that fusion companies have raised a total $14.24 billion. The report highlights record annual investment, growing commercial partnerships, and confidence that fusion machines will deliver electricity in the 2030s.
https://techcrunch.com/2026/07/15/why-realta-fusion-is-building-a-fusion-reactor-at-an-old-hot-dog-factory/?mc_cid=111d3f3090&mc_eid=UNIQIDWhy&mc_cid=d4e3182122&mc_eid=UNIQID Realta Fusion is building a fusion machine at an old hot dog factory
Realta Fusion selected Madison, Wisconsin's former Oscar Mayer site as the location for its new headquarters and facility. They plan to build their prototype magnetic mirror fusion machine here, with the project expected to create 600+ jobs and a hub for fusion innovation.

In March, we hosted a virtual workshop with Miko Matsumura on one of the most important topics for startup founders:
How Founders Actually Raise Money from VCs
The response was overwhelmingly positive.
Many attendees told us it was the most valuable fundraising workshop they had ever attended, and that it fundamentally changed how they think about raising venture capital.
ThatтАЩs why weтАЩre bringing it back.
Join Us on August 13
WeтАЩre excited to welcome Miko Matsumura for a second fundraising masterclass, where heтАЩll share the practical insights heтАЩs gained from more than 25 years in Silicon Valley as a founder, operator, and investor.
Miko has:
Raised $50M+ in venture capital as a founder
Invested in companies such as OpenSea
Led investments at gumi Cryptos Capital
Served as Chief Developer Evangelist at Sun Microsystems
During this interactive session, Miko will cover:
ЁЯТб What makes a startup truly venture-backable

Whether youтАЩre raising a Pre-Seed, Seed, or Series A round, this workshop can help you avoid months of costly trial and error.
WeтАЩll also leave plenty of time for live Q&A, so you can get your fundraising questions answered directly by Miko.
Because we want to keep the discussion interactive, seating is limited.
How to Register
Whether youтАЩre in Seattle, Tokyo, Munich, Sydney, Beijing, or anywhere else in the world, youтАЩre welcome to join us live. ЁЯМН
We look forward to helping more founders level up their fundraising game.

messages.snippet: View this post on the web at https://www.founderfridays.co/p/founder-fridays-no-203 Happy Firday! Did a savvy founder share this with you? Subscribe below. Build a Governance Fortress, Not Just Founder Control Founder control is the weakest
messages.text:
View this post on the web at https://www.founderfridays.co/p/founder-fridays-no-203
Happy Firday!
Did a savvy founder share this with you? Subscribe below.
Build a Governance Fortress, Not Just Founder Control
Founder control is the weakest way to protect your startupтАЩs mission because a single person is a guardian with an expiration date, and success makes you vulnerable by turning your company into a target. Eric Ries writes in Incorruptible that this is why Whole Foods couldnтАЩt resist Amazon despite being profitable every quarter it was public. the real defense is a governance fortress, a five-step structural system starting with a Public Benefit Corporation foundation, then a supermajority vote requirement of two-thirds of ALL shares (not just votes cast) so an activist has to win active consent from two-thirds of every shareholder, then a self-reinforcing lock that requires the same two-thirds vote to change the two-thirds rule itself, then a board mission pledge to keep directors focused on mission, and finally outer walls like a classified board, high proxy barriers, and a poison pill. Costco built this in 1985 and held off every attack since. Twilio learned the hard way. when founder Jeff LawsonтАЩs supervoting shares sunset, it took activist investors less than 199 days to force him out because the company had built nothing to protect the mission once founder control expired. The fortress outlives the founder. Forbes [ https://substack.com/redirect/4399e6db-424e-4a8a-966b-e4a645e27dc8?j=eyJ1IjoiN2EzYzFhIn0.gmSjAtdlofMqOFUjgfQgnPyMBlCD_j00mTQEinip6mM ] (12 minutes)
What Should Founders Never Automate?
Founders are rushing to automate everything with AI, but 95% of AI projects fail to deliver ROI according to MITтАЩs study of 300 enterprise deployments, and the problem has nothing to do with the tools. The real issue is that most AI budgets went to visible functions like sales and marketing while the biggest measured returns came from invisible back-office operations. A seed-stage founder automated his entire customer support pipeline with a 70% deflection rate and near-instant response time, then discovered six months later that the same feature complaint had been phrased forty different ways in resolved tickets that nobody had read. The AI answered every question politely, so the signal never reached a human, and he lost those customers not because the agent failed but because the answers were good enough that critical product insights disappeared. messages.text:
Atlassian
Hello,
We are writing to inform you that we are updating our legal terms, which apply to all Atlassian cloud customers, effective August┬а17,┬а2026.
>> https://www.atlassian.com/legal?utm_source=sfmc&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=ACM-14784&jobid=108164402&subid=1666557761
As part of this change, we are updating how Atlassian may use customer metadata and inтАСapp data to improve apps and AI experiences for all customers. This change currently impacts customers with an active plan or trial of Jira, Confluence, and Jira┬аService Management.
>> https://support.atlassian.com/security-and-access-policies/docs/what-types-of-data-does-my-organization-contribute/?utm_source=sfmc&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=ACM-14784&jobid=108164402&subid=1666557761
As of July┬а10,┬а2026, our records indicate that you do not currently have an active plan or trial of Jira, Confluence, and/or Jira┬аService Management; therefore, there is no impact or change to how Atlassian uses the data in your Atlassian apps at this time.
To learn more, you can review the updated terms on our legal site. If you have any questions, please contact customer support or your Atlassian representative. You may also refer to our website to learn more about cancellation if you choose not to accept the updated terms.

messages.subject: Supa Update July 2026
messages.snippet: Everything that happened in the last month at Supabase Everything that happened in the last month at Supabase Supabase ***************************** Supabase Update тАФ July 2026 ***************************** Hey there ЁЯСЛ, HereтАЩs everything t
messages.text:
Everything that happened in the last month at Supabase
Everything that happened in the last month at Supabase
Supabase
*****************************
Supabase Update тАФ July 2026
*****************************
Hey there ЁЯСЛ,
HereтАЩs everything that happened with Supabase in the last month:
OpenCode integrates with Supabase
---------------------------------
OpenCode integrates with Supabase
---------------------------------
OpenCode connects your agent to your Supabase database, Edge Functions, and logs, with MCP setup configured automatically.
Read the blog ( https://supabase.com/blog/agentic-coding-on-supabase-with-opencode ) тЖТ
TanStack DB syncs with Supabase
-------------------------------
TanStack DB syncs with Supabase
-------------------------------
@supabase-labs/tanstack-db syncs TanStack DB collections with your Supabase tables over PostgREST and Realtime, and is available in alpha.
Watch the demo ( https://x.com/supabase/status/2069429278253498562 ) тЖТ
Wrappers MongoDB foreign data wrapper
--------------------------------------------
Wrappers adds a MongoDB foreign data wrapper
--------------------------------------------
Wrappers v0.6.2 lets you query and join MongoDB collections directly from Postgres.
Read the docs ( https://supabase.com/docs/guides/database/extensions/wrappers/mongodb ) тЖТ
Multigres LISTEN/NOTIFY
----------------------------------------------------------
Multigres supports LISTEN/NOTIFY across pooled connections
----------------------------------------------------------
Multigres keeps Postgres LISTEN/NOTIFY working even when connections are pooled away from clients.
Read the blog ( https://multigres.com/blog/2026-05-24-listen-notify ) тЖТ
Realtime Broadcast binary payloads
-------------------------------------------
Realtime Broadcast supports binary payloads
-------------------------------------------
Realtime Broadcast can now send and receive binary payloads in addition to JSON, which cuts encoding overhead for sensor telemetry and live screenshot streaming.
Read the docs ( https://supabase.com/docs/guides/realtime/broadcast ) тЖТ
---------------------------
Quick product announcements
---------------------------
Updates
* The Postgres log_connections setting now defaults to off for new projects on all tiers starting July 9, and existing Free and Pro projects will be migrated to the new default. [GitHub Discussion ( https://github.com/orgs/supabase/discussions/47197 )]

** Join us for a mixer discussion about early startup development on July 23!
------------------------------------------------------------
From Prototype to Proof: Finding Real Traction Before You Feel Ready
тАЛJoin us for an evening exploring what it takes to turn early progress into real momentum.
Building a great product isnтАЩt enough. The founders who break through are the ones who can clearly communicate why their work mattersтАФand back that story with meaningful evidence.
Join us for an evening with Gabriel Leoni, founder of Wallrise and member of HumanmadeтАЩs 2025 Accelerator cohort, and Christopher Williams, startup advisor and commercialization mentor, as they share two complementary perspectives on moving from prototype to opportunity.
Drawing from their own experiences working with early-stage companies, theyтАЩll discuss how founders can recognize meaningful traction, build credibility through customer engagement, and develop a narrative that resonates with customers, partners, investors, and supporters alike. YouтАЩll hear how early signalsтАФwhether theyтАЩre pilot users, conversations, partnerships, or even setbacksтАФcan become part of a compelling story that helps open doors.
Whether youтАЩre still refining your first prototype or beginning to build market momentum, this session will help you think differently about the relationship between traction, storytelling, and trust.
Doors open at 4:30 PM for networking with founders, builders, and members of the Humanmade community. Presentations begin at 5:00 PM, followed by additional time to connect and continue the conversation until 7:00 PM.

messages.text:
Private decision making dinners, my talk tonight in SF, and the H1 2026 report
Hi everyone, It was quite the first half of the year. The fundraising bar moved тАУ most founders found out the hard way, and most investors quietly changed how they source and screen. Agents are between us. We've spent these months in the middle of it: closed-door dinners, vetting calls, and hundreds of conversations on both sides of the table. Here's what's ahead тАУ and two things you can grab this week. The Season of Private Dinners тАУ Fast Track to Decision Makers Why would a general partner show up to a private dinner in the middle of summer? Not for the food. GPsтАЩ and MPsтАЩ time is the most limited asset in the fund, they often send venture partners, associates and analysts to demo days, networking events, and pitch nights тАУ the person who signs stays home. They show up in person for one thing тАУ deal flow that's already been vetted. That's the program we're hosting in Silicon Valley over the next three months тАУ a series of private dinners by niche: AI & Enterprise тАУ July 30 Health, Bio & Impact тАУ August 11 DeepTech, Infra & Robotics тАУ September 8 Consumer & Entertainment тАУ late September Five vetted startups at one table. 10-15 decision-makers across from them тАУ GPs, managing partners, principals and family-office CIOs, hand-picked for each niche and actively deploying capital right now. No analysts, no scouts. In short: we provide a fast track to 15 active decision-makers in your vertical. ЁЯСЙ Founder? Apply for a seat: go.synexis.io/prem For investors: every deal at the table is vetted before you ever see it тАУ we review and fine-tune each company, then a partner fund vets the deal independently. And everyone seated next to you can also say yes тАУ the room is decision-makers only. ЁЯСЙ Request a seat at the table: go.synexis.io/prinv Tonight in San Francisco тАУ The New Fundraising Bar I'm on stage tonight (Thursday, July 16, around 7 PM, SF Financial District) at the founders & investors evening hosted by Zahava Stroud and Angel Launch, with a talk I've wanted to give for months: ┬лThe New Fundraising Bar: What Actually Changed in 2026┬╗. Ten minutes of what founders actually need to know right now тАУ the concentration, the DPI freeze, why the old playbook stopped working. It comes from real deal flow, not theory. ------------------------------------------------------------
We are excited to announce that the building formerly known as Omni Commons is now Revival Hall: a place where culture, movement, and community resilience unite under one roof.  Revival means more than repair, it means bringing new life to a place with deep roots and a long history of bringing people together.
We couldn't have asked for a better inaugural event than Soul Source (https://www.instagram.com/soulsource.la/) , which gave Revival Hall its first heartbeat with an unforgettable night of music, movement, and community. More than 200 people filled the dance floor as DJs Nina Sol, Mark de Clive-Lowe, Brooklyn Terry, and blksoap set the tone for a new chapter in the buildingтАЩs history.
The evening marked the launch of our Cultural Event Series, which will continue through the end of the year with programming that brings people together through art, music, and ceremony. If youтАЩre looking for a community-run space to host a mutual aid event, celebration of life, milestone, fundraiser, workshop, or gathering, weтАЩd love to hear from you. Click the button below to inquire about booking Revival Hall.
Our team has been hard at work rehabilitating the building to re-activate it for regular community use including: hiring staff, installing a new roof, repainting the building, and setting up building security.
Want to support Revival Hall? EB PREC is currently raising $10M to support the next phase of our work, which includes rehabilitating more of Revival Hall for community activation. Keep reading to learn about our capital campaign and how you can support Revival HallтАЩs next chapter!

messages.subject: Relay.app is shutting down
messages.snippet: An important update on Relay.app Today we have difficult news to share: Relay.app will be shutting down on August 15, 2026 for free users and September 14, 2026 for paying customers. WeтАЩve worked closely with many of you for years, and we k
messages.text:
An important update on Relay.app
Today we have difficult news to share: Relay.app will be shutting down on August 15, 2026 for free users and September 14, 2026
for paying customers.
WeтАЩve worked closely with many of you for years, and we know this change will be disruptive. We are very sorry for the work this
will cause you, and we donтАЩt take it lightly, so our top priority is to make this transition as seamless as possible.
What happens today
 * New signups and free-to-paid upgrades are now turned off.
 * Everything you already use will continue working through the end of the wind-down window. Your workflows will keep running.
 * You can export your data starting now (see 'Taking your data with you').
If you're on a free plan
You have 30 days from today to keep using Relay.app and to export your data. After Sat Aug 15 at 23:59 PT, free accounts and all
associated data will be permanently deleted.
If you're a paying customer
 * You will receive an email from help@paddle.com that your subscription is cancelled, effective today. This means that you will
   not be charged again, but you will continue to be able to use Relay.app for free until Mon Sep 14 at 23:59 PT.
 * For annual customers, we will automatically initiate a prorated refund for the unused remainder of the current billing period.
   You should receive this refund within 5 business days. Your past invoices are available here
   [https://customer-portal.paddle.com/cpl_01hfv834x8nx1jdne0eyhrzjb1].
 * You keep full access to Relay.app, for free, for 60 days тАФ through Mon Sep 14 at 23:59 PT.
 * On top of your current plan, your workspace will receive an additional 25,000 steps and 10,000 AI credits per month for the
   next 60 days. This is free to help with the transition. You will not be charged for any usage during this period.
 * After Mon Sep 14 at 23:59 PT, paid accounts and all associated data will be permanently deleted.
Taking your data with you
We have made it as straightforward as possible to take the important parts of your workspace with you. You can export:
 * Workflows, Sequences and MCP servers as JSON and AI prompts so you can rebuild them elsewhere
 * Run history
 * Tables as CSV files
There are more details on how to export data here [https://docs.relay.app/workspace-and-account/export-your-relay.app-data].
Your data and your privacy
Customer content and product data that you do not export will be permanently deleted at the end of the wind-down window,
consistent with our commitme

messages.text:
View this post on the web at https://ossoff.substack.com/p/a-personal-request
ItтАЩs Jon. IтАЩm personally asking if you can chip in $26 to my reelection campaign today.
If youтАЩre outraged by whatтАЩs happening to this country тАФ and youтАЩre ready to fight for the future of our Republic тАФ please donтАЩt wait: Rush $26 to my reelection campaign in Georgia right now. [ https://substack.com/redirect/dba51a66-abe0-4c16-bb3a-672fcb14d540?j=eyJ1IjoiMjA3YTNlIn0.f9_phVLAepuQSeTJ5XFFT77Dec15zCHN03ISyRPmwU4 ]
Otherwise, IтАЩd like to share some numbers IтАЩm thinking about as my team and I prepare our strategy to ensure DECISIVE victory in the midterms тАФ one so overwhelming and undeniable that it is impossible for Trump and MAGA to deny.
$11 million: ThatтАЩs how much has already been spent to defeat me in this U.S. Senate race. All before the general election even started.
$44 million: ThatтАЩs how much the Senate GOPтАЩs main Super PAC has pledged to spend against me. A record-breaking sum from them at this stage in the race here in Georgia.
ЁЯдпЁЯдпЁЯдп$269 million: ThatтАЩs how much money Republicans spent trying to defeat me in 2020 and 2021. I expect them to spend even more this year.
$26: ThatтАЩs the average online donation to our campaign in July, and itтАЩs what IтАЩm personally asking you to contribute today [ https://substack.com/redirect/dba51a66-abe0-4c16-bb3a-672fcb14d540?j=eyJ1IjoiMjA3YTNlIn0.f9_phVLAepuQSeTJ5XFFT77Dec15zCHN03ISyRPmwU4 ] to defend this seat.
1.2%: That was the margin of victory when we won this Georgia Senate seat in 2021. It was *that* close. And we were only able to do it because supporters like you chipped in the resources we needed to win.
Just like last time, with your support we will win again in Georgia and together we will save our Republic.
Please use this link to add a donation тАФ of $26 or whatever you can afford тАФ today.  [ https://substack.com/redirect/dba51a66-abe0-4c16-bb3a-672fcb14d540?j=eyJ1IjoiMjA3YTNlIn0.f9_phVLAepuQSeTJ5XFFT77Dec15zCHN03ISyRPmwU4 ]Thank you. messages.text:
View this post on the web at https://www.acquiredbriefing.com/p/from-nfl-to-startup-coo-to-congressman
KyleтАЩs Rating: 8/10
While I typically find AcquiredтАЩs interview episodes less compelling than their company deep-dives, GonzalezтАЩs journey from NFL receiver to DeFi-experimenting Congressman made for a surprisingly fresh conversation. His hands-on approach to understanding crypto before regulating it, combined with the rare perspective of someone whoтАЩs actually built startups and understands technology while serving in Congress, made this far more engaging than I expected.
Did an Acquired fan share this with you? Subscribe below.
Anthony Gonzalez
Anthony Gonzalez is a former NFL wide receiver, startup COO, and sitting Republican U.S. Congressman from OhioтАЩs 16th district. He serves on the House Financial Services Committee (overseeing crypto/Web3 regulation, the Fed, and financial services), the Science, Space, and Technology Committee (including NASA oversight), and the Climate Committee. His significance stems from his rare combination of sports, tech startup, and venture-backed experience, making him one of the few Congress members who actively uses and understands crypto/DeFi while shaping its regulation.
The episode focuses on GonzalezтАЩs nonlinear career: from Cuban immigrant family roots in Northeast Ohio, to catching passes from Peyton Manning, to running a venture-backed startup, to entering Congress during 2016тАЩs polarization. It emphasizes his pivotal decisions (impeaching Trump after January 6, pushing crypto legislation), personal anecdotes (family steel business amid manufacturing decline, MichiganтАЩs recruiting snub leading to Ohio State), and standout moments (experimenting with DeFi to inform policy, voting against party on impeachment). The narrative frames Gonzalez as a pragmatic, urgency-driven leader representing тАЬsophisticated gritтАЭ in Northeast Ohio, balancing district economics with national issues like defending democracy and American dynamism.
Notable Facts
GonzalezтАЩs family fled Cuba post-revolution; his grandfather rejected CastroтАЩs court position offer, hid in jungles before legally immigrating to Ohio, where they restarted through old school connections in Cincinnati.
Played WR at Ohio State (recruited after Michigan ignored him, mistaking him for a kicker); drafted first-round by Colts, caught TDs from Peyton Manning, but injuries ended career after Patriots stint.

messages.text:
Silicon Valley Doesn't Do Summer - Startup Grind July Update
Hi all,Silicon Valley doesn't slow down for summer. If anything, this is when the best founders do their sharpest work while everyone else is on the beach. This month's roundup has something for founders at every stage: an almost sold-out flagship event, a format change worth showing up for, an AI agent that can incorporate your company in one prompt, and a new international program looking for mentors.Female Founder Series, 5th Edition, Shack15, SFWe're close to sold out again. We've moved to a bigger room at Shack15 just to keep up with demand. Top-tier VC speakers, and the most promising female founders in software, AI, and hardware across the Bay Area.We're also expanding to New York City this September, partnering with Inc. Magazine.тЖТ Grab one of the final tickets AI Demo Series тАФ July 28, New FormatJuly's edition goes open mic. Half the presenters are drawn at random from who's checked in, so if you're in the room, you've got a real shot at the stage. Show up in person, not just on the list.тЖТ RSVP for a chance to presentRamp for Agents: Idea to Incorporated Company in One PromptStartup Grind runs on Ramp, and we're fans. But this isn't just for us. It's for founders at any stage, including pre-launch. Ramp's new AI agent takes you from "I have an idea" to an operating company: US incorporation and financial ops set up, in a single prompt.
